004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
020
021import java.util.*;
022
023/**
024 * A fluent accessor for a named HTTP response header.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 * Provides convenient typed-access methods for reading header values.
028 * Returns {@code null} or empty results when the named header is absent from the response.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * Obtain instances via {@link RestResponse#header(String)}.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
035 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
036 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
037 * (and possibly earlier).
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
040 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
041 * </ul>
042 *
043 * @since 9.2.1
044 */
045@SuppressWarnings({
046   "resource" // Eclipse resource analysis: response is borrowed; caller closes it
047})
048public final class ResponseHeader {
049
050   private final String name;
051
052   private final RestResponse response;
053
054   ResponseHeader(String name, RestResponse response) {
055      this.name = name;
056      this.response = response;
057   }
058
059   /**
060    * Returns the header name.
061    *
062    * @return The header name. Never <jk>null</jk>.
063    */
064   public String getName() {
065      return name;
066   }
067
068   /**
069    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this header is present in the response.
070    *
071    * @return <jk>true</jk> if present.
072    */
073   public boolean isPresent() {
074      return response.getFirstHeader(name) != null;
075   }
076
077   /**
078    * Returns the first value of this header, or <jk>null</jk> if absent.
079    *
080    * @return The header value, possibly <jk>null</jk>.
081    */
082   public String getValue() {
083      var h = response.getFirstHeader(name);
084      return h != null ? h.value() : null;
085   }
086
087   /**
088    * Returns the first value of this header, or the given default if absent.
089    *
090    * @param defaultValue The default value to return when the header is absent.
091    * @return The header value, or {@code defaultValue} if absent.
092    */
093   public String orElse(String defaultValue) {
094      var value = getValue();
095      return value != null ? value : defaultValue;
096   }
097
098   /**
099    * Returns the first value of this header as an {@link Optional}.
100    *
101    * @return An optional containing the first header value, or empty if absent.
102    */
103   public Optional<String> asOptional() {
104      return o(getValue());
105   }
106
107   /**
108    * Returns the first value of this header parsed as an {@code int}, or {@code -1} if absent or not parseable.
109    *
110    * @return The integer value, or {@code -1} if absent or not an integer.
111    */
112   public int asInteger() {
113      var value = getValue();
114      if (value == null)
115         return -1;
116      try {
117         return Integer.parseInt(value.trim());
118      } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
119         return -1;
120      }
121   }
122
123   /**
124    * Returns the first value of this header parsed as a {@code long}, or {@code -1L} if absent or not parseable.
125    *
126    * @return The long value, or {@code -1L} if absent or not a long.
127    */
128   public long asLong() {
129      var value = getValue();
130      if (value == null)
131         return -1L;
132      try {
133         return Long.parseLong(value.trim());
134      } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
135         return -1L;
136      }
137   }
138
139   /**
140    * Returns all values of this header.
141    *
142    * <p>
143    * For repeated headers, this returns each header value in encounter order.
144    *
145    * @return An unmodifiable list of header values. Never <jk>null</jk>, but possibly empty.
146    */
147   public List<String> getValues() {
148      return response.getHeaders().stream()
149         .filter(h -> name.equalsIgnoreCase(h.name()))
150         .map(TransportHeader::value)
151         .toList();
152   }
153
154   /**
155    * Returns the first value as a comma-split list of individual tokens.
156    *
157    * <p>
158    * Useful for headers like {@code Accept} or {@code Allow} that combine multiple values with commas.
159    *
160    * @return A list of trimmed tokens. Never <jk>null</jk>, but possibly empty.
161    */
162   public List<String> asCsvList() {
163      var value = getValue();
164      if (value == null)
165         return List.of();
166      return Arrays.stream(value.split(","))
167         .map(String::trim)
168         .filter(s -> !s.isEmpty())
169         .toList();
170   }
171
172   @Override /* Object */
173   public String toString() {
174      var value = getValue();
175      return value != null ? name + ": " + value : name + ": <absent>";
176   }
177}
